questions.prompt: Complete: "The ___ of the new bridge took three years." (base word: CONSTRUCT)
questions.explanation: The gap follows the article "The" and needs a noun: construction (-tion suffix). "Constructive" is an adjective meaning helpful or productive. "Constructing" is a present participle or gerund — it could head a noun phrase ("The constructing of…") but is far less natural than "construction" here. "Constructed" is a past participle/adjective and cannot serve as the head noun after "The".

questions.prompt: Which negative prefix correctly forms the opposite of 'logical'?
questions.explanation: The prefix il- attaches to roots beginning with l: il- + logical = illogical. "Unlogical" uses the wrong prefix — un- is for general adjectives (unhappy, unreliable) and does not collocate with this Latin-origin adjective. "Mislogical" and "inlogical" are not words in standard English.

questions.prompt: Complete: "Her ___ handling of the crisis impressed the board." (base word: SKILL — adjective form)
questions.explanation: The gap precedes a noun ("handling") and follows a possessive ("Her"), so an adjective is needed: skilful (-ful suffix). "Skill" is a noun and cannot directly modify another noun in this slot without forming a compound ("skill handling" is not natural). "Skilfully" is an adverb and cannot modify a noun. "Skillness" is not a word — the correct noun form is "skill" or "skilfulness".

questions.prompt: Find the sentence with a word-formation ERROR.

options.text: The committee's inability to reach consensus caused delays.
Her remarkably precise analysis won widespread admiration.
The govern of the region introduced sweeping reforms.
Irregular working hours affected their productivity.

questions.explanation: Option (c) uses "govern" (a verb) where a noun is required: The government (or governor) of the region. Options (a), (b), and (d) all use correctly formed words: inability (in- + able + -ity), remarkably (remark + -able + -ly as an adverb), and irregular (ir- + regular) and productivity (produce + -ivity).

questions.prompt: Complete: "The ___ of the witnesses made the trial far more complex." (base word: CONTRADICT — use the NOUN form)
questions.explanation: The gap follows "The" and needs a noun (plural, as there are multiple witnesses): contradictions (-tion + plural -s). "Contradictory" is an adjective. "Contradicting" is a present participle/gerund — it could head a gerund phrase but is less precise here as a count noun. "Contradictively" is not a standard word — the adverb form is "contradictorily".

questions.prompt: Complete both gaps: "The scheme was praised for its ___ design and its ___ impact on local communities." (gap 1: base INNOVATE — adjective; gap 2: base TRANSFORM — adjective)
questions.explanation: Both gaps precede nouns ("design", "impact") and need adjectives. Gap 1: innovative (-ive suffix from innovate). Gap 2: transformative (-ative suffix, meaning having the power to transform). Option (a): "innovation" is a noun and cannot modify "design" attributively; "transforming" is a present participle — possible but less precise than "transformative" at C1. Option (c): both are adverb and past participle — neither can precede a noun as an attributive adjective here. Option (d): "innovating" is a present participle — awkward before a noun; "transformational" is a valid adjective but "transformative" is the standard C1 collocation for this context.
